_note: Narrow, well-scoped hotfix: floating-point truncation in settlement calculation. Used by eval 1 to test branch naming and slug derivation (step 4 only).

bug.title: Bug: Settlement amount rounds incorrectly for amounts above 10,000
bug.body:
## What is broken?

Merchants receiving settlement payouts for amounts above £10,000 are consistently underpaid by £0.01. The issue has been traced to `calculateSettlementAmount()` in the settlement service, which uses JavaScript floating-point division.

## User impact

Revenue impact: all merchants processing settlements above £10,000. Approximately 200 settlements per day are affected, each short by £0.01.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create a settlement for £10,000.01
2. Observe `calculateSettlementAmount()` returns £10,000.00

## Expected behaviour

Settlement amount should match the exact pence value, with no floating-point rounding error.

## Actual behaviour

Settlement amount is £0.01 less than expected for any amount above £10,000.
